title: quercus adriatica
intro: Quercus adriatica, also known as the Dalmatian white oak, is a deciduous tree species native to the Dalmatia region in Croatia. The bark of this tree contains tannins, which contribute to its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Quercus adriatica include its potential to improve cardiovascular health, reduce inflammation, and protect against certain infections. The herbal preparations of Quercus adriatica often involve the use of its bark in the form of infusions, decoctions, or extracts, which can be used to treat various health conditions.
